items.title: Conditional independence assumption
items.description: Multinomial and Bernoulli NB inherit the naive factorization P(x|y) = ∏ P(x_i|y); this topic specifies the text feature types and likelihood forms that plug into that assumption.

items.title: Multinomial and Bernoulli distributions
items.description: Class-conditional word models follow multinomial draws over counts (Multinomial NB) or independent Bernoulli trials per word presence (Bernoulli NB).

items.title: Log scores and cross-entropy
items.description: Training and prediction use sums of log probabilities; negative log posterior scores align with cross-entropy-style losses over predicted class distributions.

items.title: From bag-of-words baselines to neural text models
items.description: Naive Bayes classifiers with explicit word probabilities are classical NLP baselines; modern LLMs learn contextual representations but still optimize log-probability objectives at scale.
